revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:橋本市の歴史
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name=Shinodajinja |image=[[File: Shinodajinjya Honden.jpg |260px]]
Shinodajinja Main Hall (信太神社本殿) (November 2020) |Location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]] Kuju 283 |LatitudeDegrees=34 |LatitudeMinutes=20|LatitudeSeconds=11.1 |LongitudeDegrees=135|LongitudeMinutes=33|LongitudeSeconds=13.4 |LabelPosition=left |Map=Japan Wakayama |EnshrinedDeities=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]]
[[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]]
{{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been {{ill|enshrined|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjō|qq=}} from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|qq=}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|qq=}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|qq=}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto City]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:橋本市の建築物]] [[Category:橋本市の歴史]]

Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto City]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]]

{{Shrine |Name=Shinodajinja |image=[[File: Shinodajinjya Honden.jpg |260px]]
Shinodajinja Main Hall (信太神社本殿) (November 2020) |Location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]] Kuju 283 |LatitudeDegrees=34 |LatitudeMinutes=20|LatitudeSeconds=11.1 |LongitudeDegrees=135|LongitudeMinutes=33|LongitudeSeconds=13.4 |LabelPosition=left |Map=Japan Wakayama |EnshrinedDeities=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]]
[[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]]
{{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|qq=}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|qq=}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto City]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]]

The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|qq=}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|qq=}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:和歌山県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]]

This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q105945047]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name=Shinodajinja |image=[[File: Shinodajinjya Honden.jpg |260px]]
Shinodajinja Main Hall (信太神社本殿) (November 2020) |Location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]] Kuju 283 |LatitudeDegrees=34 |LatitudeMinutes=20|LatitudeSeconds=11.1 |LongitudeDegrees=135|LongitudeMinutes=33|LongitudeSeconds=13.4 |LabelPosition=left |Map=Japan Wakayama |EnshrinedDeities=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]]
[[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]]
{{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|qq=}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|qq=}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|qq=}}. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= |prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|qid=Q842098}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.336416666666665|N|135.55372222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|qq=}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|qq=}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shinta Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|qq=|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shinta Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= |prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|qid=Q842098}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.336416666666665|N|135.55372222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|qq=|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Shinta Shrine]]

According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|qq=|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Shinta Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q105945047]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name=Shinodajinja |image=File:Shinodajinjya Honden.jpg |Location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]] Kuju 283 |LatitudeDegrees=34 |LatitudeMinutes=20|LatitudeSeconds=11.1 |LongitudeDegrees=135|LongitudeMinutes=33|LongitudeSeconds=13.4 |LabelPosition=left |Map=Japan Wakayama |EnshrinedDeities=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]]

[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= |prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|qid=Q842098}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.336416666666665|N|135.55372222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|qq=|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}}

According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|qq=|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}}

The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|qq=|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}}

[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= |prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|WD=Q842098}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.336416666666665|N|135.55372222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}}

At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}}

revision.text:
[[ja:信太神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q105945047]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name=Shinodajinja |image=File:Shinodajinjya Honden.jpg |Location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]] Kuju 283 |LatitudeDegrees=34 |LatitudeMinutes=20|LatitudeSeconds=11.1 |LongitudeDegrees=135|LongitudeMinutes=33|LongitudeSeconds=13.4 |LabelPosition=left |Map=Japan Wakayama |EnshrinedDeities=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]]
[[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]]
{{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= |prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|WD=Q842098}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.336416666666665|N|135.55372222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= |prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|WD=Q842098}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.336416666666665|N|135.55372222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
