[[ja:信太神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q105945047]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name=Shinodajinja |image=File:Shinodajinjya Honden.jpg |Location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]] Kuju 283 |LatitudeDegrees=34 |LatitudeMinutes=20|LatitudeSeconds=11.1 |LongitudeDegrees=135|LongitudeMinutes=33|LongitudeSeconds=13.4 |LabelPosition=left |Map=Japan Wakayama |EnshrinedDeities=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]]
[[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]]
{{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= |prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|WD=Q842098}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.336416666666665|N|135.55372222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:信太神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q105945047]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name=Shinodajinja |image=File:Shinodajinjya Honden.jpg |Location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]] Kuju 283 |LatitudeDegrees=34 |LatitudeMinutes=20|LatitudeSeconds=11.1 |LongitudeDegrees=135|LongitudeMinutes=33|LongitudeSeconds=13.4 |LabelPosition=left |Map=Japan Wakayama |EnshrinedDeities=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]]
[[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]]
{{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= |prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|WD=Q842098}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.336416666666665|N|135.55372222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}}

In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}}

[[ja:信太神社]] [[ja:信太神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q105945047]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name=Shinodajinja |image=File:Shinodajinjya Honden.jpg |Location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]] Kuju 283 |LatitudeDegrees=34 |LatitudeMinutes=20|LatitudeSeconds=11.1 |LongitudeDegrees=135|LongitudeMinutes=33|LongitudeSeconds=13.4 |LabelPosition=left |Map=Japan Wakayama |EnshrinedDeities=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]]
[[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]]
{{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= |prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|WD=Q842098}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.336416666666665|N|135.55372222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:信太神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}}

This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:信太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:信太神社]] [[ja:信太神社]] [[ja:信太神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q105945047]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name=Shinodajinja |image=File:Shinodajinjya Honden.jpg |Location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]] Kuju 283 |LatitudeDegrees=34 |LatitudeMinutes=20|LatitudeSeconds=11.1 |LongitudeDegrees=135|LongitudeMinutes=33|LongitudeSeconds=13.4 |LabelPosition=left |Map=Japan Wakayama |EnshrinedDeities=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]]
[[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]]
{{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= |prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|WD=Q842098}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.336416666666665|N|135.55372222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:信太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. [[ja:信太神社]] [[ja:信太神社]] [[ja:信太神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q105945047]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name=Shinodajinja |image=File:Shinodajinjya Honden.jpg |Location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]] Kuju 283 |LatitudeDegrees=34 |LatitudeMinutes=20|LatitudeSeconds=11.1 |LongitudeDegrees=135|LongitudeMinutes=33|LongitudeSeconds=13.4 |LabelPosition=left |Map=Japan Wakayama |EnshrinedDeities=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]]
[[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]]
{{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival=[[:en:October 15|October 15]] |MainHall= |prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|WD=Q842098}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.336416666666665|N|135.55372222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]][[:en:Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|Takano-guchi Town]]. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:信太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. The [[:en:Camphora officinarum|camphor tree]] on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]]. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in [[:en:979|979]] (the 2nd year of [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen]]) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been [[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]] from the [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]]’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (February of the 42nd year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Imperial Deity]] (アマテラス) * [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto]] (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto]] * [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanaga Hime]] (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 [[:en:Metre|m]] and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of [[:en:Ilex rotunda|Kurogane Mochi]] with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, [[:en:1959|1959]] (the 34th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa Period]]), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:信太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=1909}} (February of the 42nd year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Imperial Deity}} (アマテラス) * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto}} (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto}} * {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanaga Hime}} (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of {{ill|Ilex rotunda|en|Ilex rotunda|WD=Q844306|lt=Kurogane Mochi}} with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, {{ill|1959|en|1959|WD=Q5302|lt=1959}} (the 34th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa Period}}), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:信太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=camphor tree}} on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument}}. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in {{ill|979|en|979|WD=Q23784|lt=979}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|Tengen (era)|en|Tengen (era)|WD=Q1327101|lt=Tengen}}) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement}} from the {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}}’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=1909}} (February of the 42nd year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Imperial Deity}} (アマテラス) * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto}} (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto}} * {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanaga Hime}} (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 {{ill|Metre|en|Metre|WD=Q11573|lt=m}} and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of {{ill|Ilex rotunda|en|Ilex rotunda|WD=Q844306|lt=Kurogane Mochi}} with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, {{ill|1959|en|1959|WD=Q5302|lt=1959}} (the 34th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa Period}}), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:信太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto}}
{{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanaga Hime}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival={{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15}} |MainHall= |prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|WD=Q842098}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.336416666666665|N|135.55372222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|en|Hashimoto, Wakayama|WD=Q842098|lt=Hashimoto City}}{{ill|Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|en|Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|WD=Q5313447|lt=Takano-guchi Town}}. The {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=camphor tree}} on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument}}. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in {{ill|979|en|979|WD=Q23784|lt=979}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|Tengen (era)|en|Tengen (era)|WD=Q1327101|lt=Tengen}}) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement}} from the {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}}’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=1909}} (February of the 42nd year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Imperial Deity}} (アマテラス) * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto}} (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto}} * {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanaga Hime}} (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 {{ill|Metre|en|Metre|WD=Q11573|lt=m}} and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of {{ill|Ilex rotunda|en|Ilex rotunda|WD=Q844306|lt=Kurogane Mochi}} with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, {{ill|1959|en|1959|WD=Q5302|lt=1959}} (the 34th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa Period}}), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:信太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 {{ill|Metre|en|Metre|WD=Q11573|lt=m|1=Metre}} and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of {{ill|Ilex rotunda|en|Ilex rotunda|WD=Q844306|lt=Kurogane Mochi|1=Ilex rotunda}} with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, {{ill|1959|en|1959|WD=Q5302|lt=1959|1=1959}} (the 34th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa Period|1=Shōwa era}}), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:信太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:信太神社]] [[ja:信太神社]] [[ja:信太神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name=Shinodajinja |image=File:Shinodajinjya Honden.jpg |Location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|en|Hashimoto, Wakayama|WD=Q842098|lt=Hashimoto City|1=Hashimoto, Wakayama}}{{ill|Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|en|Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|WD=Q5313447|lt=Takano-guchi Town|1=Kōyaguchi, Wakayama}} Kuju 283 |LatitudeDegrees=34 |LatitudeMinutes=20|LatitudeSeconds=11.1 |LongitudeDegrees=135|LongitudeMinutes=33|LongitudeSeconds=13.4 |LabelPosition=left |Map=Japan Wakayama |EnshrinedDeities={{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Imperial Deity|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}}
{{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanaga Hime|1=Iwanaga-hime}} |ShrineRank={{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Foundation=Unknown |AnnualFestival={{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} |MainHall= |prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|WD=Q842098|1=Hashimoto, Wakayama}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.336416666666665|N|135.55372222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Shinodajinja''' (しのだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|en|Hashimoto, Wakayama|WD=Q842098|lt=Hashimoto City|1=Hashimoto, Wakayama}}{{ill|Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|en|Kōyaguchi, Wakayama|WD=Q5313447|lt=Takano-guchi Town|1=Kōyaguchi, Wakayama}}. The {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=camphor tree|1=Camphora officinarum}} on the shrine grounds (the camphor tree of Shinodajinja) is a prefecturally designated {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument|1=Natural monument}}. ==概要== This shrine is regarded as the guardian deity for the regions of Kuju, Uenaka, Tahara, and Shimonaka. Although its origins and history remain unknown, it is said that in {{ill|979|en|979|WD=Q23784|lt=979|1=979}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|Tengen (era)|en|Tengen (era)|WD=Q1327101|lt=Tengen|1=Tengen (era)}}) the worship hall, offering hall, treasury, and tower gate were rebuilt. According to tradition, the enshrined deities were also said to have been {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}} from the {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}}’s {{ill|Seishinja (Izumi City)|ja|聖神社 (和泉市)|lt=Shinta no Mori|lt_ja=信太の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishinja (Izumi City)|1=Hijiri Shrine (Q11611103)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611103}}. Locally, the shrine is known as the deity who seals away moles ({{ill|Mole|ja|モグラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モグラ科|1=Talpidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104825}}), with local lore claiming that moles have become extinct in the area. In {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=1909|1=1909}} (February of the 42nd year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}), it attained the status of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === 境内 === * Main Hall (housing 5 shrines; wooden structure with a copper-plated roof; 1.62m2){{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 |title=信太神社 |access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県神社庁}} * Shrine Office (wooden, tiled-roof, single-story structure; 25.9m2) ==祭神== * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Imperial Deity|1=Amaterasu}} (アマテラス) * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Tenshin Hiko-Funahiko no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} (ニニギ) * {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Josasai Hiyori no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒早日尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} (ニギハヤヒ) * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamiyawainarehiko no Mikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanaga Hime|1=Iwanaga-hime}} (イワナガヒメ) ==天然記念物== [[File: Shinodajinjya Kusu.jpg|thumb|220px|信太神社の樟樹]] According to late Edo Period local geography records such as the {{ill|Kii Continuation Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Continuation Fudoki|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}, it is noted that "within the precincts there stands a great camphor tree (クス) surrounded by more than three jō in circumference, bearing witness to the ancient history of the shrine grounds." This camphor tree is a grand specimen with an approximate height of 25 {{ill|Metre|en|Metre|WD=Q11573|lt=m|1=Metre}} and a trunk circumference of about 7 m. Estimated to be around 400 years old, the tree still boasts a luxuriant canopy with its vigor hardly diminished. At the base of its main trunk, a venerable specimen of {{ill|Ilex rotunda|en|Ilex rotunda|WD=Q844306|lt=Kurogane Mochi|1=Ilex rotunda}} with a trunk circumference of 2.1 m has become completely fused with the camphor tree’s root, forming a rare natural phenomenon.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.hashimoto.lg.jp/guide/kyoikuiinkai/syougaku/bunka/bunnkazai/wakayamaken_siteibunkazai/omobun/1359698878625.html|publisher=橋本市|title=信太神社の樟樹|accessdate=2021-2-11}} On January 8, {{ill|1959|en|1959|WD=Q5302|lt=1959|1=1959}} (the 34th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa Period|1=Shōwa era}}), the camphor tree of Shinodajinja was designated a Natural Monument by the prefectural authorities.現地案内看板、橋本市教育委員会、平成5th year 3月設置{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/500700/mokuroku/kenkinenbutsu.html |title=県指定文化財・記念物（信太神社の樟樹）|access-date=2022-10-21 |publisher=和歌山県教育委員会}} ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4039 Shinodajinja] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|信太神社|version=97792769|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:信太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q105945047}} [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
